tools.name: query_iocs
tools.description: Query the TweetFeed API for Indicators of Compromise (IOCs: URLs, domains, IPs, MD5/SHA256 hashes) shared by the infosec community on Twitter/X. Returns matching rows with date, researcher handle, type, value, tags, and tweet URL. All data CC0 licensed. The 'year' time window is not supported here (

tools.name: check_url
tools.description: Check whether a URL (or substring) appears in the TweetFeed corpus over the past 30 days. Useful for confirming if an observed URL has been flagged by the public infosec Twitter/X community. Case-insensitive substring match against the 'value' field of type=url IOCs. Returns matching rows with date,

tools.name: check_ip
tools.description: Check whether an IP address appears in the TweetFeed corpus. Exact match over the past 365 days (falls back to a 30-day substring window if there's no exact hit, so '1.2.3' will still match '1.2.3.4' there). Useful for confirming if an observed IP has been flagged as attacker infrastructure (C2, sca

tools.name: check_hash
tools.description: Check whether a file hash (MD5 or SHA-256) appears in the TweetFeed corpus. Exact match over the past 365 days (falls back to a 30-day window if there's no exact hit). Useful for confirming if a binary sample has been shared by the public infosec Twitter/X community. Hash type auto-detected from len

tools.name: list_recent_iocs
tools.description: List TweetFeed IOCs added since a given date, useful for delta-syncing a blocklist or Threat Intelligence pipeline. Source is the 30-day month window so 'since' must be within the past 30 days; older queries return only the part within the month window. Optional 'type' and 'tag' filters narrow the r

tools.name: get_tag_info
tools.description: Bundle of TweetFeed activity for a single tag: aggregate counts across today/week/month/year windows plus the most recent IOCs. Saves the agent from making three separate calls to assemble a tag overview. Tag can be passed with or without a leading '#'.

tools.name: get_trending
tools.description: Top tags and IOC-type distribution for a given time window, computed from the live counts.json aggregate. Useful for 'what is the infosec community talking about right now' or 'which malware family is spiking this week' queries. Source: GET https://api.tweetfeed.live/v1/counts (regenerated every 15

tools.name: enrich_ioc
tools.description: Look up an IOC value in TweetFeed. First an EXACT lookup over the past 365 days (aggregated: first_seen, last_seen, count, reporters, tags, last source tweets; accepts defanged input and http/https variants), including AI-generated context (summary, malware family, threat type) when available. If no

tools.name: get_campaigns
tools.description: AI-clustered campaign groupings of the last 7 days of community-shared TweetFeed IOCs: each campaign bundles related URLs/domains/IPs/hashes under a name, a short context summary, a clustering confidence (high/medium/low), and a targeted brand when one was identified, plus a sample of member IOCs. R

tools.name: get_trends
tools.description: IOC trend analytics from the last 31 days: daily volume by type, top moving tags week-over-week, most-abused TLDs, new vs recurring indicator ratio, and feed producer concentration.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp.tweetfeed.live" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: check_url, get_campaigns, get_trends · Private data: check_hash · Public sink: query_iocs, check_url.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: check_url, get_campaigns, get_trends · Private data: check_hash · Public sink: query_iocs, check_url

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "check_ip" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Check whether an IP address appears in the TweetFeed corpus over the past 30 days. Useful for confirming if an observed IP has been flagged as attacker infrastructure (C2, scanner, phishing host) by the public infosec Twitter/X community. Substring match against the 'value' field of type=ip IOCs (so" → after: "Check whether an IP address appears in the TweetFeed corpus. Exact match over the past 365 days (falls back to a 30-day substring window if there's no exact hit, so '1.2.3' will still match '1.2.3.4' there). Useful for confirming if an observed IP has been flagged as attacker infrastructure (C2, sca"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "check_hash" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Check whether a file hash (MD5 or SHA-256) appears in the TweetFeed corpus over the past 30 days. Useful for confirming if a binary sample has been shared by the public infosec Twitter/X community. Hash type auto-detected from length (32 hex = MD5, 64 hex = SHA-256). Exact match on hex value, case-i" → after: "Check whether a file hash (MD5 or SHA-256) appears in the TweetFeed corpus. Exact match over the past 365 days (falls back to a 30-day window if there's no exact hit). Useful for confirming if a binary sample has been shared by the public infosec Twitter/X community. Hash type auto-detected from len"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "get_trending" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.
